label: GEOS XML authoring as eval target (36-task easy-setting suite)
rationale: Task set mined from GEOS tutorial RSTs; easy setting provides all values; ground truth XML available. Well-established by prior repo1/repo2/repo3 work.

label: XMLTreeSim as headline metric (0-1, reported as x10)
rationale: Replaces arbitrary weighted composite; respects tree structure; handles element multiplicity via bipartite matching; per-section sub-scores fall out naturally. Implemented in src/eval/judge_geos.py.

label: Docker-per-task sandbox + hardlink contamination filter + RAG blocklist
rationale: Two-layer enforcement: filesystem-level hardlink tree bind-mounted ro + MCP-server-level excluded-source filter. Variant stem expansion + example_pairs.jsonl RST mapping. Audited via eval_metadata.json per task.

label: GEOS-RAG plugin (3-DB ChromaDB + geos-rag skill) as candidate adaptation surface
rationale: Custom RAG ported from repo1/repo2 with 3 indices: documentation (RST), XML files, schema (input-schema + datastructure definitions). Per-task ChromaDB copy + MCP preflight. Whether this helps CC vs. CC's built-in file browsing is the open empirical question.

label: ablation_deepseek_v2: no-plugin CC + deepseek-v3.2 on 36 tasks
rationale: Apples-to-apples no-plugin baseline using the v2 harness (primer-in-system-prompt, --disallowedTools Skill). 33/36 success, 3 timeout.
decision_reason: Serves as the reference no-plugin condition for all plugin-vs-baseline contrasts on deepseek-v3.2.

label: repo3_eval_run2: plugin CC + minimax-m2.7 on 36 tasks
rationale: Earlier plugin run scored at 0.809 mean TreeSim, 78%% at >=7. Model differs from ablation baseline so this alone does NOT isolate plugin contribution.
decision_reason: Useful as plugin-with-stronger-model reference point, but confounded vs E01.

label: RQ: find CC adaptation that beats vanilla CC at model parity
rationale: Paper contribution rests on demonstrating some adaptation wins. First order of business for this session.

label: repo3_eval_run4: plugin CC + deepseek-v3.2 on 36 tasks
rationale: Apples-to-apples vs E01: same 36 tasks, same deepseek-v3.2 model, same 20-min timeout. Scored 2026-04-20 against experiments_gt. 1/36 failed scoring (ExampleSPE11b parse error). Paired comparison on common 35 tasks shows plugin wins on 29/35, loses on 6, mean delta +0.175, pass >=0.7 rate 88.6% vs 54.3%.
decision_reason: Headline positive result for the paper: custom RAG plugin demonstrably improves CC on the easy GEOS-XML task at model parity. Resolves the main gap flagged in ablation_findings.md.

label: Memory-system adaptation (Mem0 / Cheatsheet / BoT / GMemory; frozen pre-learned preferred)
rationale: User's strongest bet. Frozen pre-learned favored over continual because it preserves parallelism. Can be implemented external-to-CC (prompt-prepended cheatsheet) or as an MCP tool.

label: Primer design variations (structure, scope, in-prompt vs workspace file)
rationale: Ablation findings soft-suggest primer-in-system-prompt reduces timeouts. Instruction/structure variations untested. Bordering on vanilla-CC /init behavior so 'novelty' is thin — useful as a supporting result only.

label: File-tree context injection (pre-computed GEOS tree in system prompt)
rationale: Empirically CC wastes turns on ls/glob in inputFiles and docs. A static tree listing in the system prompt could cut those tool calls.

label: tree_run1: plugin + precomputed inputFiles tree (~4500 tokens) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Injected a compact index of 746 XML files across 87 subdirs of /geos_lib/inputFiles into system prompt. Hypothesis: removes Glob/Bash-find exploration burn; gives agent direct file references. Result: 17/17 scored (3 timeouts produced partial XML that scored), 0 redacted_thinking failures. Mean TreeSim 0.604 vs plugin-only 0.831 paired, delta -0.227. Loses on 15/17 tasks. Largest losses on EXACTLY the tasks plugin-only rescues most (Sneddon -0.719; DPWellbore -0.687; Mandel -0.637). Filetree seems to distract agent from RAG-driven workflow.
decision_reason: System-prompt injection of any auxiliary content (cheatsheet OR filetree) hurts plugin. Plugin's value is specifically the RAG MCP + skill prompt; further system-prompt augmentation is counterproductive. Strong cross-experiment pattern.

label: minprimer_run1: plugin + minimal primer (~450 tokens vs full ~10K) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Primer ablation: does primer content carry weight. Result: DECISIVELY YES. Minimal primer scored mean 0.596 vs full-primer plugin 0.831 paired delta -0.235. 16/17 tasks regress. 4 timeouts (vs 0 in E03). Largest losses on same 3 tasks as E07 (Sneddon -0.723, DPWellbore -0.688, Mandel -0.677). Primer content is load-bearing — the plugin's value is only fully realized with detailed primer context. Full primer + RAG MCP together form the active-ingredient stack.
decision_reason: Minimal primer is strictly worse. Full primer is load-bearing. Surprising finding: even with RAG present, the primer's detailed documentation map and schema-navigation context are needed for the rescue mechanism to fire on weak-model tasks.

label: memws_run1: plugin + abstract cheatsheet in /workspace/CHEATSHEET.md (NOT system prompt) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Delivery-channel test: cheatsheet_abstract.md (550 tokens; shortcuts + pitfalls) copied to /workspace/CHEATSHEET.md; system prompt has only a 2-line pointer. Result: 11/17 agent success (vs 17/17 E03), 6 timeouts, 1 ParseError, 1 FileNotFoundError. Paired 15 scored: memws 0.622 vs plugin-only 0.831 delta -0.212. Wins 2/15, loses 13/15. Same rescue-task regressions as E07/E08 (Sneddon -0.733, DPWellbore -0.696, Mandel -0.618). Workspace-file delivery is NOT a safe channel for memory on this stack.
decision_reason: Channel distinction doesn't help. The failure is deeper than delivery — perturbing the tightly-coupled primer+plugin state consistently breaks the rescue mechanism on the same 3 tasks. Only remaining memory channel to test: MCP tool (E11).

label: Simpler flat-chunking RAG as alternative to the 3-DB custom design
rationale: User's own note: maybe the 3-DB design was overengineered; plain chunked doc RAG might be a fairer 'standard RAG' ablation point.

label: Memory-on-top-of-plugin: stack frozen pre-learned cheatsheet over existing plugin (demonstrates adaptation stacking)
rationale: E03 shows plugin already wins. Next research question: does adding memory yield compounding gains? Design: hold out N tasks as train set, CC+plugin writes lessons-learned cheatsheet, freeze cheatsheet, evaluate on remaining test tasks with cheatsheet prepended to system prompt. Preserves parallelism.

label: mem_run1: plugin + cheatsheet CC + deepseek-v3.2 on 17 test tasks
rationale: First memory-stacking experiment. Cheatsheet (~1700 tokens, 6 themed sections) generated from 18 held-out train trajectories of repo3_eval_run4 via deepseek lesson-extraction + aggregation. Test set: 17 paired tasks. Result: MEMORY DECISIVELY HURT PERFORMANCE. 13/17 success (vs 17/17 in plugin baseline); 3 tasks failed with no XML output, 1 timed out. On 14 paired-scorable tasks, memory mean TreeSim 0.532 vs plugin 0.831 (delta -0.322). Memory wins on 1/14, loses on 13/14. Including 3 no-output tasks as 0, overall mean 0.438 (delta -0.393). Dominant failure mode: agent emits 'redacted_thinking' block then end_turn after 3-6 turns without calling Write/Edit. Hypothesis: cheatsheet bloat past ~23K system-prompt tokens triggers premature stop in deepseek-v3.2, and task-specific advice (e.g., 'use InternalWellbore') from train-task patterns mismatches test-task requirements.
decision_reason: Frozen-cheatsheet approach as specified in D-001 does not work on deepseek-v3.2. The hypothesis of 'adaptation stacking' is NOT supported by this design. Dead end for cheatsheet-in-system-prompt; revisit memory via (a) shorter cheatsheet restricted to cross-task patterns only, (b) memory as MCP tool rather than system-prompt inject, (c) stronger model that handles longer context.

label: Plugin decomposition: geos-rag skill vs MCP RAG server attribution
rationale: Current plugin bundles (a) geos-rag skill prompt/instructions and (b) 3-DB ChromaDB MCP server. To attribute the E03 gain, need to run plugin-without-RAG-MCP (skill only) and RAG-MCP-without-skill. Defer to week-scope unless time.

label: gmem_run1: plugin + G-Memory-lite MCP tool (memory_lookup) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Frozen memory index of 18 train trajectories delivered as an MCP tool memory_lookup(query). Agent called the tool on 17/17 tasks (1 call each) — tool is discovered and used. Paired delta -0.192 vs plugin-only (0.638 vs 0.831 on 17). 13/17 success, 4 timeouts, wins 1/17 (pkn +0.142), loses 16/17. DIFFERENT from other negatives: memory RESCUES DPWellbore (0.939 vs plugin 0.992, only -0.05) and IMPROVES pkn. But still fails Sneddon -0.716, Mandel -0.669. Smallest negative delta in the memory battery (-0.192 vs -0.212 to -0.322 for E04/E05/E07/E08/E09).
decision_reason: MCP-tool channel is the least-bad memory variant but still net negative on the 17-task test subset. The rescue mechanism on Sneddon + Mandel appears fragile to any perturbation regardless of delivery channel. Memory-via-MCP is not strictly dead - it DOES rescue some tasks where other memory variants fail - but overall hurts.

label: memshort_run1: plugin + short cross-task-only cheatsheet (~300 tokens) + explicit stop criterion on 17 test tasks
rationale: Isolates content-specificity (hypothesis 2) from context-bloat (hypothesis 1) for the E04 failure. New short cheatsheet: ~300 tokens, RAG usage + XML structure + common mistakes + explicit stop criterion. Result: 13/17 success (vs 17/17 plugin-only, matching E04's 13/17); mean TreeSim 0.561 on 14 scored vs 0.831 plugin-only on 17 (E03 test subset) - still catastrophically worse. Failure pattern identical to E04: same redacted_thinking -> end_turn signature on ModifiedCamClay (trapped in failed_no_outputs) and ExtendedDruckerPrager (new failure); and 2 timeouts (EDPWellbore, Mandel - both succeeded in both plugin-only and E04 long-cheatsheet conditions). Conclusion: length is NOT the dominant cause of cheatsheet failure. Any cheatsheet-in-system-prompt triggers the failure mode on a subset of tasks.
decision_reason: Confirms hypothesis 1 (context bloat alone) is insufficient to explain the E04 failure. Both long (1700t) and short (300t) cheatsheets show same failure pattern. Defers memory via system-prompt-inject design entirely; future memory should be MCP-tool-on-demand or on a different model.

label: mm_noplug_run1: minimax-m2.7 no-plugin on 17 test tasks (cross-model paired vs E02)
rationale: Cross-model robustness confirmed. 17/17 agent success in 17 min wall (workers=12, no rate-limit issues). Scored 15/17 (2 minimax tasks wrote malformed XML - ParseErrors on ExampleThermalLeakyWell and TutorialPoroelasticity). Paired on 15 tasks vs E02 (plugin+minimax): minimax no-plugin 0.694 vs minimax+plugin 0.809, delta -0.115 paired. Plugin wins 11/15, loses 4. Cross-model generalization of plugin advantage CONFIRMED on minimax-m2.7, though delta (-0.115) smaller than on deepseek-v3.2 (-0.175). Suggests plugin helps more on weaker base models.
decision_reason: Positive cross-model result. Strengthens paper claim: plugin advantage is not a deepseek artifact. Concurrency=12 workers confirmed safe for OpenRouter rate limits.
